mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and cash-flow multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. The signal is rejected if rate cuts do not coincide with higher 20-day returns than flat or rising-rate periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher in months when FEDFUNDS is falling versus months when it is flat or rising, or if triggered periods show negative median forward returns, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation print that accelerates above the prior month signals a tighter real-rate backdrop and valuation compression, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when FEDFUNDS is not falling at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases do not precede negative or below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign disappears when FEDFUNDS is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates slackening labor demand and weaker future earnings growth, so sharp unemployment increases should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The relationship fails when unemployment rises because of a temporary shock while output and sales remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment jumps greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the median return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue growth for index constituents, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should be weaker when retail sales are rising only because of inflation rather than real demand, which would show up as CPIAUCSL accelerating faster than RSAFS.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth does not predict above-zero 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes after inflation-adjusting with CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and better earnings expectations for cyclicals, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if output gains do not translate into higher forward returns once the cycle is mature and rates are rising.
failure_condition: Reject if positive trends in INDPRO fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling hit rate is not above 50% in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply, housing demand is contracting and interest-sensitive activity is weakening, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the decline coincides with a rising policy rate, because higher borrowing costs amplify the slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE means payroll growth is slowing while unemployment rises, confirming broad labor-market weakening that should lead S&P 500 lower over the next week. If only one labor series weakens without the other, the signal is too noisy and should fail.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or if either labor variable alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP level trend in GDPC1 combined with firm RSAFS indicates broad final-demand strength that supports earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when GDP is rising but sales are not, because output gains are then inventory or trade driven rather than demand driven.
failure_condition: Reject if joint rises in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if GDP strength without sales strength produces the same predictive power.
